215	Skill contents:
216	
217	- Trigger: any writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing of Markdown under
218	  `docs/`.
219	- Required workflow:
220	  1. preserve the author's intent and DDx terminology;
221	  2. edit the document;
222	  3. run `ddx doc prose --changed`;
223	  4. apply high-signal findings;
224	  5. rerun `ddx doc prose --changed`;
225	  6. summarize remaining intentional exceptions.
226	- Preservation rules:
227	  - keep paths, commands, IDs, frontmatter, headings, table structure, API
228	    names, and acceptance criteria intact;
229	  - do not flatten useful technical density;
230	  - do not rewrite quoted source text unless the task is explicitly about that
231	    quote.
232	- Rewrite guidance:
233	  - replace broad claims with actor/action/artifact/evidence;
234	  - delete filler transitions when the sentence still reads correctly;
235	  - prefer shorter wording when it is equally precise;
236	  - split overstuffed sentences only when doing so improves reviewability;
237	  - leave a finding unresolved when the flagged text is precise and explain why.
238	- Examples:
239	  - unsupported benefit claim before/after;
240	  - AI-slop paragraph before/after;
241	  - token-cost reduction before/after with word-count delta;
242	  - false positive that should be ignored;
243	  - table/path/API sample that must not be rewritten.
244	
245	Skill evals:
246	
247	| Eval | Prompt | Pass condition |
248	|---|---|---|
249	| `docs-edit-runs-check` | Edit a small doc under `docs/`. | Agent runs `ddx doc prose --changed` after editing. |
250	| `fixes-ai-slop` | Given a doc with broad LLM-default claims. | Agent rewrites to concrete actor/action/evidence and reruns the check. |
251	| `preserves-technical-structure` | Given a doc with paths, commands, tables, IDs, and AC. | Agent leaves structure intact and does not chase false positives. |
252	| `reduces-token-cost` | Given filler-heavy prose with clear meaning. | Agent shortens prose without losing constraints or evidence. |
253	| `reports-exceptions` | Given a legitimate finding that should remain. | Agent states the exception and rationale instead of silently ignoring it. |
254	
255	How we know the skill works:
256	
257	- The eval prompts run through the same skill-loading path agents use in normal
258	  work.
259	- Each eval checks behavior, not exact prose: command was run, findings were
260	  handled, structure was preserved, and remaining exceptions were explained.
261	- A failed eval produces a concrete skill patch, not a checker-rule patch,
262	  unless the underlying deterministic finding is wrong.
263	
264	### Phase 6: Workflow Hooks
265	
266	Run prose checks in normal DDx work without making users ask.
267	
268	Work:
269	
270	- When a `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempt changes Markdown under `docs/`, run
271	  `ddx doc prose --changed` before finalization.
272	- Include prose findings in execution evidence.
273	- Feed findings back to the agent before final response when the attempt is
274	  still editable.
275	- Keep correctness review and prose findings separate.
276	- Keep pre-commit advisory-only unless a later ADR changes policy.
277	
278	Acceptance:
279	
280	- Docs-changing attempts attach prose-check evidence.
281	- Advisory prose findings do not block bead closure by default.
282	- Agents get findings early enough to fix obvious prose issues.
283	- Operator-facing output stays concise.
284	
285	## Rule Strategy
286	
287	DDx should use Vale for deterministic matching and Markdown awareness, but DDx
288	owns the quality model. Rules should target document usefulness, not generic
289	style preferences.
290	
291	Initial rule families:
292	
293	| Rule family | Flags | Does not flag |
294	|---|---|---|
295	| Unsupported broad claim | "robust", "comprehensive", "seamless", "powerful" when no concrete actor/action/evidence follows | measured claims, concrete test matrices, named capabilities |
296	| AI-slop construction | fluent polish that omits actor, action, artifact, boundary, or evidence | precise summary of concrete behavior |
297	| Filler transition | "it is important to note", "in today's landscape", "to be clear" when removable | quoted source text, intentional public copy if still specific |
298	| Missing actor/action/artifact | sentence says a thing "enables" or "supports" without naming what changes | API descriptions with explicit subject and behavior |
299	| Token-cost reduction | removable intensifiers, throat-clearing, duplicate summary sentences | necessary definitions, acceptance criteria, legal/security constraints |
300	
301	## Token-Cost Policy
302	
303	Lower word count is beneficial when it preserves or improves specificity. The
304	checker should prefer edits that remove filler and duplicate phrasing, but it
305	must not compress away:
306	
307	- acceptance criteria;
308	- command examples;
309	- file paths and IDs;
310	- rationale needed for future review;
311	- constraints that prevent agent drift;
312	- evidence that supports a decision.
313	
314	The evaluation corpus should track token-cost candidates separately from
315	clarity findings so DDx can tune this without encouraging terse but ambiguous
316	docs.
317	
318	## Bead Breakdown
319	
320	This plan is not one bead. It is an epic with dependency-ordered child beads.
321	Each child should be executable on its own, with a narrow file scope and a
322	small verification surface.
323	
324	Some child beads require higher-judgment execution: corpus labeling, rule
325	semantics, skill behavior, and workflow-hook policy. Those beads should use the
326	bead-level execution-hint policy in TD-037 rather than ad hoc model or harness
327	choices. In particular, `power:smart` requires an inline `SMART JUSTIFICATION`
328	in the bead description, and durable harness/provider/model pins are out of
329	scope for this plan.
330	
331	### Epic
332	
333	`prose: integrate Vale-backed DDx prose quality`
334	
335	Acceptance:
336	
337	1. `ddx doctor` reports pinned Vale health.
338	2. `ddx doc prose --changed` uses Vale-backed DDx rules with no project
339	   `.vale.ini`.
340	3. Normalized findings use DDx rule IDs, rationales, and suggested edits.
341	4. Labeled corpus tests prove useful findings and low structural noise.
342	5. `human-writing-support` tells agents how to run and apply the workflow.
343	6. Docs-changing DDx attempts attach advisory prose-check evidence.
344	
345	### Child Beads
346	
347	| Order | Bead | Scope | Suggested hint | Depends on |
348	|---:|---|---|---|---|
349	| 0 | `try: define and enforce bead-level execution hints` | Implement TD-037 parsing, lint, evidence, and metrics slices needed before smart hints are relied on. | `power:smart` with TD-037 justification | none |

350	| 1 | `doctor: validate pinned Vale prose checker` | `cli/cmd/doctor.go`, doctor tests, constants for Vale version. | none | 0 |
351	| 2 | `docprose: add DDx Vale style pack skeleton` | `library/checks/prose-quality/styles/DDx/`, metadata schema, no command wiring. | none | 1 |
352	| 3 | `docprose: add corpus harness for normalized findings` | `cli/internal/docprose/testdata/corpus/`, corpus loader/tests, expected JSON schema. | `power:smart` for corpus design judgment | 2 |
353	| 4 | `docprose: port initial rules to Vale styles` | Vale style files and corpus golden cases for unsupported claim, AI slop, filler, token cost. | `power:smart` for rule precision judgment | 3 |
354	| 5 | `docprose: generate temporary Vale config` | config generation from DDx defaults/project config; no Vale execution yet. | none | 2 |
355	| 6 | `docprose: invoke Vale and parse JSON` | Vale subprocess adapter, JSON structs, error diagnostics. | none | 5 |
356	| 7 | `docprose: normalize Vale findings to DDx findings` | rule-id mapping, rationale/suggested-edit mapping, line merge behavior. | `power:smart` for finding-quality semantics | 4, 6 |
357	| 8 | `doc prose: switch command to Vale-backed engine` | `ddx doc prose --changed` and explicit path behavior. | none | 7 |
358	| 9 | `bead review: reuse Vale-backed prose findings` | `ddx bead review --prose` path, review evidence. | `power:smart` for review-policy interaction | 8 |
359	| 10 | `skills: upgrade human-writing-support workflow examples` | active and shipped skill copies, eval prompts if available. | `power:smart` for skill behavior quality | 8 |
360	| 11 | `try: attach prose-check evidence for docs-changing attempts` | execute/try/work evidence capture and advisory handling. | `power:smart` for workflow-hook policy | 8, 10 |
361	| 12 | `docs: run Vale-backed prose pass across DDx docs` | Apply high-signal findings to `docs/**`; record before/after finding count and word-count delta. | `power:smart` for corpus-scale editorial judgment | 11 |
362	
363	### Split Rules
364	
365	- If a bead would touch both command plumbing and rule semantics, split it.
366	- If a bead would require changing execution flow and docs content, split it.
367	- If corpus labels change because rule behavior changes, keep that with the
368	  rule bead that caused the change.
369	- Do not file the full-doc cleanup bead until the checker, skill, and workflow
370	  evidence path are working.
371	
372	Each bead should reference ADR-025 and this plan via `spec-id` or inline
373	context. The first implementation bead should start with `ddx doctor` because
374	the engine decision depends on a trustworthy Vale binary.

294					var unavailable bool
295					if selection, selectErr := profileSelector.Select(ctx, inferredPolicy, requestMinPower); selectErr == nil && selection.Name != "" {

336				}

338					return reportFromResult(res), nil
339				}
340				if execErr != nil {
341					agent.MarkResultExecutionError(res, execErr)

343				}
344				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {

350						checksRel := filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
351						anyFailed, ratchetFailed, evalErr := agent.EvaluateRequiredGatesForResult(wt, ids, res, projectRoot, checksAbs, checksRel)
352						if evalErr != nil {
353							fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "ddx: warning: gate evaluation failed: %v (skipping)\n", evalErr)
354						} else if anyFailed || ratchetFailed {
355							preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)

358							} else {

360								res.Outcome = "preserved"
361								if ratchetFailed {
362									res.Reason = agent.RatchetPreserveReason
363								} else {
364									res.Reason = "post-run checks failed"
365								}
366								res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
367								res.Detail = agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
368							}
369							_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)

371							if inferredPolicy != "" {

373							}
374							report := agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, policyStr)
375							report.InferredPowerClass = string(inferredPolicy)

411				}

477			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
478			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
479		}

481		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
482			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
483		}
484		report := result.Results[0]
485		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
486		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.InferredPowerClass != "" {
487			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: powerClass=%s source=%s\n", intent.InferredPowerClass, intent.Source)
488		}
489	
490		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
491	}
492	
493	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
494		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
495		if report.Detail != "" {
496			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
497		}
498		if report.ResultRev != "" {
499			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
500		}
501		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
502			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
503		}
504	}
505	
506	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
507	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
508		switch status {
509		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
510			return nil // exit 0
511		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
512			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
513			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
514		default:
515			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
516			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
517		}
518	}
519	
520	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
521	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
522	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
523		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
524		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
525			return nil
526		}
527		var unmet []string
528		for _, depID := range depIDs {
529			dep, err := store.Get(context.Background(), depID)
530			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
531				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
532			}
533		}
534		return unmet
535	}
536	
537	// singleBeadStore wraps a *bead.Store and overrides ReadyExecution to return
538	// only a single target bead. All other operations are delegated unchanged.
539	// This allows the execute-bead worker to treat the forced bead as if it were
540	// the only entry in the execution-ready queue.
541	type singleBeadStore struct {
542		*bead.Store
543		target bead.Bead
544	}
545	
546	func (s *singleBeadStore) ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error) {
547		return []bead.Bead{s.target}, nil
548	}

113	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
114		mode := r.Mode
115		if mode == "" {
116			if r.Once {
117				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
118			} else {
119				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
120			}
121		}
122		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
123		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
124			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
125		}
126		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
127			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
128		}
129		return mode, idleInterval
130	}

161	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
162	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
163	// worker.
164	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute

167	// where no configured powerClass can currently run the bead. This is transient
168	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
169	// triage:needs-investigation park.
170	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

172	// SmartRetryCooldown is the queue-fairness pause after a real no_changes
173	// implementation attempt asks for status=open/smart retry. The bead remains
174	// open with its powerClass hint, but a watch worker moves on instead of repeating the

176	const SmartRetryCooldown = 5 * time.Minute

178	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
179	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
180	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
181	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
182	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
183	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
184	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
185	//
186	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
187	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
188	//
189	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
190	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
191	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
192	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
193	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
194	// directly.
195	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
196		ctx context.Context,
197		projectRoot string,
198		b *bead.Bead,
199		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
200		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
201		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
202		actor, source string,
203		now func() time.Time,
204	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
205		if now == nil {
206			now = time.Now
207		}
208		if submit == nil {
209			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
210		}
211		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
212		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
213		if err != nil {
214			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
215			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
216			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
217			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
218			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
219				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
220			}
221			return &LandResult{
222				Status:      "preserved",
223				PreserveRef: ref,
224				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
225			}, nil, nil
226		}
227		if outcome.Blocked {
228			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
229			if perr != nil {
230				return nil, outcome, perr
231			}
232			if eventStore != nil {
233				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
234			}
235			return land, outcome, nil
236		}
237		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
238			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
239		}
240		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
241		return land, outcome, sErr
242	}

16	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
17	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
18	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
19	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
20	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
21	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
22		cmd := &cobra.Command{
23			Use:   "work",
24			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
25			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
26	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
27	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
28	
29	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
30	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
31	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
32	bounds.
33	
34	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
35	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
36	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
37	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
38	
39	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
40	  drained     — no ready beads remain
41	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
42	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
43	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
44	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
45	
46	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
47	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
48	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
49	`,
50			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
51	  ddx work
52	
53	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
54	  ddx work --once
55	
56	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
57	  ddx work --watch
58	
59	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
60	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
61	
62	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
63	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
64	
65	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
66	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
67	
68	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
69	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
70			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
71			RunE: f.runWork,
72		}

119	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
120	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
121	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
122	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
123	// by this layer.
124	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
125		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128	
129		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
130		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
131	
132		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
133		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
134		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
135		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
136		if !asJSON {
137			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
138			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
139				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
140			})
141		}
142	
143		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
144	}

146	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
147		if asJSON {
148			payload := struct {
149				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
150				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
151			}{
152				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
153				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
154			}
155			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
156			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
157			return enc.Encode(payload)
158		}
159	
160		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
161			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
162			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
163			return nil
164		}
165	
166		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
167		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
168		if result.Failures > 0 {
169			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
170			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
171				if attempt.Status != "success" {
172					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
173					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
174						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
175					}
176					fmt.Fprintln(w)
177				}
178			}
179		}
180		if result.NoReadyWork {
181			fmt.Fprintln(w)
182			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
183			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
184		}
185		return nil
186	}

188	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
189		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
190		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
191		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
192			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
193	}
194	
195	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
196		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
197			switch attempt.Status {
198			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
199				closed++
200				changed++
201			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
202				closed++
203				alreadySatisfied++
204			}
205		}
206		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
207			closed = result.Successes
208		}
209		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
210	}

212	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
213		switch result.ExitReason {
214		case "drained":
215			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
216		case "once_complete":
217			return "once complete"
218		case "budget":
219			return "budget reached"
220		case "no_progress":
221			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
222		case "blocked":
223			return "blocked waiting for external action"
224		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
225			return "stopped by signal"
226		case "fatal_config":
227			return "fatal configuration error"
228		case "preflight_failed":
229			return "preflight failed"
230		case "resource_exhausted":
231			return "resource exhausted"
232		case "routing_unavailable":
233			return "routing unavailable"
234		}
235		if result.StopCondition != "" {
236			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
237		}
238		return "completed"
239	}

250	// checkForUpdates performs automatic update check (synchronous, once per 24h)
251	func (f *CommandFactory) checkForUpdates(cmd *cobra.Command) {
252		// Check if disabled via env var
253		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
254			return
255		}
256	
257		// Load config
258		cfg, err := config.Load()
259		if err != nil {
260			// Silent failure - use defaults
261			cfg = config.DefaultNewConfig()
262		}
263	
264		// Check if disabled via config
265		if cfg.UpdateCheck != nil && !cfg.UpdateCheck.Enabled {
266			return
267		}
268	
269		// Create checker and perform check (synchronous)
270		checker := update.NewChecker(f.Version, cfg)
271		f.updateMu.Lock()
272		f.updateChecker = checker
273		f.updateDone = make(chan struct{})
274		done := f.updateDone
275		f.updateMu.Unlock()
276	
277		// Run the check asynchronously so command startup is never blocked.
278		go func() {
279			defer close(done)
280			_, _ = checker.CheckForUpdate(context.Background())
281			// Also refresh plugin version cache while we have a network call in flight.
282			f.refreshPluginVersionCache()
283		}()
284	}

300		}

302		if done != nil {
303			select {
304			case <-done:
305			default:
306				return nil
307			}
308		}
309	
310		// Skip update notification for help commands
311		if cmd.Name() == "help" || cmd.Parent() != nil && cmd.Parent().Name() == "help" {
312			return nil
313		}
314	
315		// Skip update notification for machine-readable output formats
316		jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

319		}

331		}

335			return nil
336		}

362	// checkVersionGate blocks execution if the binary is older than the project requires.
363	// Returns an error for non-exempt commands when binary version < project's ddx_version.
364	func (f *CommandFactory) checkVersionGate(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
365		// Dev builds bypass the gate
366		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
367			return nil
368		}
369	
370		// Read project versions
371		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
372		if projectVersion == "" || projectVersion == "dev" {
373			return nil // No versions.yaml or dev project — skip
374		}
375	
376		// Exempt commands that must work even when binary is too old
377		switch cmd.Name() {
378		case "upgrade", "version", "doctor", "init", "help", "completion":
379			return nil
380		}
381	
382		// Compare: is the binary older than what the project requires?
383		needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(f.Version, projectVersion)
384		if err != nil {
385			return nil // Parse error — don't block
386		}
387		if needsUpgrade {
388			return fmt.Errorf("this project requires DDx v%s or newer (you have %s).\nRun 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary",
389				strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
390				f.Version)
391		}
392	
393		return nil
394	}

432	// displayPluginStalenessHints reads the plugin version cache and shows hints for
433	// any installed plugins that have a newer version available.
434	func (f *CommandFactory) displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
435		state, err := registry.LoadState()
436		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
437			return
438		}
439	
440		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
441		if err := cache.Load(); err != nil {
442			return // no cache yet — check runs in background, hints appear next time
443		}
444	
445		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
446			latestVersion, ok := cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name]
447			if !ok {
448				continue
449			}
450			needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(entry.Version, latestVersion)
451			if err == nil && needsUpgrade {
452				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
453					"\n💡 %s %s installed, %s available. Run 'ddx install %s' to update.\n",
454					entry.Name, entry.Version, latestVersion, entry.Name)
455			}
456		}
457	}

459	// refreshPluginVersionCache fetches the latest version for each installed plugin
460	// from GitHub and writes the result to the plugin cache. Called from the
461	// background update goroutine — failures are silent.
462	func (f *CommandFactory) refreshPluginVersionCache() {
463		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
464		_ = cache.Load() // ignore missing-file error
465	
466		if !cache.IsExpired() {
467			return
468		}
469	
470		state, err := registry.LoadState()
471		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
472			return
473		}
474	
475		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
476		updated := false
477		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
478			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
479			if err != nil {
480				continue
481			}
482			release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source)
483			if err != nil {
484				continue
485			}
486			latestVersion := strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
487			cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name] = latestVersion
488			updated = true
489		}
490	
491		if updated {
492			cache.Data.LastCheck = time.Now()
493			_ = cache.Save()
494		}
495	}

597	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
598		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
599			return
600		}
601		if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
602			return
603		}
604	
605		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
606		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
607			return
608		}
609	
610		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
611		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
612			return
613		}
614	
615		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, f.Commit)
616		if err != nil {
617			return
618		}
619		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
620		if err != nil {
621			return
622		}
623	
624		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
625			return
626		}
627	
628		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
629		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
630			return
631		}
632	
633		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
634			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
635			buildSHA, headSHA, aheadCount)
636	}
637	
638	func gitCommandOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
639		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
640		if err != nil {
641			return "", err
642		}
643		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
644	}
645	
646	func gitResolveCommit(dir, commit string) (string, error) {
647		return gitCommandOutput(dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commit+"^{commit}")
648	}
649	
650	func isDDXOriginURL(url string) bool {
651		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(url), "documentdrivendx/ddx")
652	}
653	
654	// resolveAgentSession looks up a session by ID from the agent session log.
655	// Returns nil if the session is not found or cannot be read.
656	func (f *CommandFactory) resolveAgentSession(sessionID string) *agent.SessionEntry {
657		if sessionID == "" {
658			return nil
659		}
660	
661		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir)
662		if err != nil {
663			return nil
664		}
665	
666		sessionLogDir := ".ddx/agent-logs"
667		if cfg.Agent != nil && cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir != "" {
668			sessionLogDir = cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir
669		}
670	
671		logDir := agent.ResolveLogDir(f.WorkingDir, sessionLogDir)
672		idx, ok, err := agent.FindSessionIndex(logDir, sessionID)
673		if err != nil {
674			return nil
675		}
676		if ok {
677			entry := agent.SessionIndexEntryToLegacy(idx)
678			bodies := agent.LoadSessionBodies(f.WorkingDir, idx)
679			entry.Prompt = bodies.Prompt
680			entry.Response = bodies.Response
681			entry.Stderr = bodies.Stderr
682			return &entry
683		}
684		return nil
685	}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(try): attach prose-check evidence for docs-changing attempts

Add an advisory ProseEvidenceHook that runs after a successful execute-bead
attempt and before CloseWithEvidence. When the attempt changed Markdown
files under docs/, the hook runs the embedded docprose checker on the
changed files, writes findings to the per-attempt evidence directory, and
appends a prose-check.findings event to the bead history. Findings are
advisory and do not block closure.

Wired in ddx try and ddx work via a CommandFactory override (used by
tests). Bead history sees the event before the attempt is finalized so
later attempts and reviewers can read the findings while the result is
still editable.

[ddx-4e9f610f]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

data.text:
Commit landed cleanly with all pre-commit checks green.

Summary of what was added for `ddx-4e9f610f`:

- **`cli/internal/agent/prose_evidence.go`** — `AttachProseEvidence` + `ProseEvidenceHook` type. Detects `docs/**/*.md` changes between BaseRev and ResultRev (via `git diff --name-only`), runs the embedded docprose checker on each file at ResultRev (via `git show`), writes findings JSON to `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/prose-findings.json`, and appends an advisory `prose-check.findings` bead event. Both detectors are injectable for tests.
- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`** — new `ProseEvidenceHook` field on `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`; invoked in the success branch just before `CloseWithEvidence` so findings land before finalization.
- **`cli/cmd/try.go` + `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`** — wire the hook with `ddx try` / `ddx work` source labels; default uses real git+docprose, overridable via `f.proseEvidenceHookOverride`.
- **`cli/cmd/command_factory.go`** — new `proseEvidenceHookOverride` test seam.
- **`cli/cmd/try_prose_test.go`** — the four named acceptance tests:
  - `TestTryDocsChanged_AttachesProseEvidence`
  - `TestTryNoDocsChanged_SkipsProseCheck`
  - `TestTryProseFindings_AdvisoryByDefault`
  - `TestTryProseFindings_FedBackBeforeFinalization`
